About this marking scheme
The purpose of this marking scheme is to provide teachers, learners and other interested parties
with an understanding of the assessment criteria used to assess this specific assessment.
This marking scheme reflects the criteria by which candidates should be assessed. A list of
indicative content suggests the range of economic concepts, theory, issues and arguments which
might be included in learners' answers. It is not intended to be exhaustive and learners do not have
to include all of the indicative content to reach the highest level of the mark scheme.
The level-based mark schemes sub-divide the total mark to allocate to individual assessment
objectives. A mark will be awarded for each assessment objective targeted in the question, then
totalled to give an overall mark for the question.


                              © Predicted Paper – in the style of WJEC/Eduqas
                                                       MARKING SCHEME – A520U10-1 (Predicted – Moderate)

GENERAL MARKING GUIDANCE
Positive Marking
It should be remembered that learners are writing under examination conditions and credit should
be given for what the learner writes, rather than adopting the approach of penalising for any
omissions. It should be possible for a very good response to achieve full marks and a very poor one
to achieve zero marks.
For each question there is a list of indicative content which suggests the range of economic
concepts, theory, issues and arguments which might be included in learners' answers. This is not
intended to be exhaustive and learners do not have to include all the indicative content to reach the
highest level of the mark scheme.

SECTION B
21. Using the data, explain the link between the two charts. [4]


 Ba                AO1                               AO2                              AO3
 nd

  2                                                                       2 marks
                                                                          Good analysis
                                                                          A well-developed chain of
                                                                          reasoning links the changes in
                                                                          Sterling to the movement in
                                                                          the current account balance.

  1    1 mark                          1 mark                             1 mark
       Limited knowledge               Limited application                Limited analysis
       Understanding of the            The data is referenced in the      A chain of reasoning exists
       exchange rate and/or current    answer.                            but is underdeveloped.
       account is shown.

  0    0 marks                         0 marks                            0 marks

                                                                          present.


Indicative content
AO1
The effective exchange rate measures the value of Sterling against a basket of major trading
partner currencies, weighted by trade volumes. The current account balance records trade in goods
and services plus primary and secondary income flows.
AO2
The data shows Sterling depreciating from 100 (2015) to around 83–88 by 2022–2023 – an overall
fall of over 10%. The current account deficit narrows from −5.4% (2016) to around −2.0% (2023)
over the same period, though not consistently.
AO3
A weaker pound makes UK exports relatively cheaper in foreign currency and imports relatively
more expensive in Sterling. Assuming the Marshall-Lerner condition holds (PEDx + PEDm > 1), net
exports should rise, improving the current account balance. A chain of reasoning: Sterling
depreciation → exports more competitive / imports dearer → quantity of exports rises, imports falls
→ trade balance improves → current account deficit narrows.
Allow other relevant points.


                               © Predicted Paper – in the style of WJEC/Eduqas
                                                          MARKING SCHEME – A520U10-1 (Predicted – Moderate)

22. Using the diagram, discuss whether the government should subsidise public transport
services. [7]


 Ba                 AO2                                AO3                               AO4
 nd

  3                                                                         3 marks
                                                                            Excellent evaluation
                                                                            Clear, supported judgements
                                                                            are made about the
                                                                            desirability of subsidising
                                                                            public transport and about the
                                                                            limitations of relying on the
                                                                            diagram.

  2    2 marks                           2 marks                            2 marks
       Good application                  Good analysis                      Good evaluation
       The diagram is used               Well-developed chain of            Clear counterarguments,
       effectively to support analysis   reasoning about the need for       using relevant theory, about
       and/or evaluation.                subsidies given positive           the dangers or limitations of
                                         consumption externalities.         subsidies.

  1    1 mark                            1 mark                             1 mark
       Limited application               Limited analysis                   Limited evaluation
       The diagram is referenced but     A chain of reasoning exists        Counterarguments are
       only superficially.               but is less convincing.            present but are not fully
                                                                            developed.

  0    0 marks                           0 marks                            0 marks
       No valid application.             No valid analysis.                 No valid evaluation.


Indicative content
AO2
The diagram shows that the free market equilibrium (where MPC = MPB) produces Q1. Because
MSB > MPB, the social optimum is at Q2 (where MSC = MSB). Without intervention, there is
underconsumption equal to Q2 – Q1, and a welfare loss triangle between the two equilibria.
AO3
Public transport generates positive consumption externalities: reduced congestion, lower pollution,
reduced road accidents, agglomeration benefits in cities, and improved access to employment.
Individual consumers only consider private benefit, ignoring these external benefits. A subsidy equal
to the MEB (marginal external benefit) will shift the MPC curve downwards / effective price faced by
consumers downwards, increasing consumption to Q2 and eliminating the welfare loss.
AO4
Counterarguments: subsidies have an opportunity cost in terms of forgone alternative government
spending on health, education etc. They may be funded by distortionary taxation. Estimating the
size of the externality is difficult – over-subsidisation risks government failure and overconsumption.
Subsidies can reduce operators' incentives for efficiency, leading to X-inefficiency and quality
decline. Distributional issues: wealthier urban commuters may disproportionately benefit.
Regulation, road pricing or investment in infrastructure may achieve the same externality
internalisation at lower cost. The diagram is a static model ignoring dynamic effects on innovation
and service quality.
Allow other relevant points.

23. Use the data to explain where the Nash equilibrium lies in this matrix. [4]


 Ba            AO1 (1 mark)                    AO2 (2 marks)                      AO3 (1 mark)
 nd

  2                                    2 marks
                                       Good application
                                       The matrix is used to show
                                       how each firm will be worse
                                       off if it deviates unilaterally
                                       from the equilibrium.

  1    1 mark                          1 mark                             1 mark
       Limited knowledge               Limited application                Limited analysis
       Answer shows a clear            The matrix data is used to         A clear chain of reasoning is
       understanding of the concept    identify an equilibrium.           given about how the
       of a Nash equilibrium.                                             equilibrium is reached or why
                                                                          firms do not deviate.

  0    0 marks                         0 marks                            0 marks
       No valid knowledge shown.       No valid application.              No chains of reasoning are
                                                                          present.


Indicative content
AO1
A Nash equilibrium occurs at a combination of strategies where no player can gain by unilaterally
deviating from their existing strategy. It is associated with game theory, where each participant is
pursuing their best possible strategy given the strategies of others.
AO2 / AO3
In this matrix the Nash equilibrium is (Advertise, Advertise) yielding (£30m, £30m). Testing: if Alpha
is advertising, Beta's options are £30m (Advertise) or £20m (Don't) – so Beta chooses Advertise. If
Beta is advertising, Alpha's options are £30m (Advertise) or £20m (Don't) – so Alpha chooses
Advertise. At (Advertise, Advertise) neither firm can profitably deviate.
Note that (Don't, Don't) would yield £45m each – a better joint outcome – but it is not a Nash
equilibrium because either firm can unilaterally deviate to earn £60m. The outcome is a classic
prisoners' dilemma: individual rationality produces a collectively suboptimal result. Lack of trust and
communication lead firms to choose their dominant strategy.
Allow other relevant points.


                               © Predicted Paper – in the style of WJEC/Eduqas
                                                         MARKING SCHEME – A520U10-1 (Predicted – Moderate)

24. Using the data, discuss whether such a fiscal stimulus will raise the UK's rate of
economic growth. [8]


 Ba                 AO2                               AO3                               AO4
 nd

  3                                      3 marks                           3 marks
                                         Excellent analysis                Excellent evaluation
                                         The link between the fiscal       Clear supported judgements
                                         stimulus and economic growth      are made about the likely
                                         is covered with detailed          effectiveness of fiscal
                                         chains of reasoning including     stimulus.
                                         the multiplier and AD/AS.

  2    2 marks                           2 marks                           2 marks
       Good application                  Good analysis                     Good evaluation
       The data is used effectively to   Developed chains of               Clear counterarguments
       support the analysis or           reasoning link the fiscal         showing fiscal stimulus may
       evaluation.                       stimulus to higher AD and         not raise growth.
                                         growth.

  1    1 mark                            1 mark                            1 mark
       Limited application               Limited analysis                  Limited evaluation
       The data is referenced            A chain of reasoning is           Counterarguments are
       superficially.                    present but underdeveloped.       present but superficial.

  0    0 marks                           0 marks                           0 marks
       No valid application.             No valid analysis.                No valid evaluation.


Indicative content
AO2
The data shows real GDP growth slowing to 0.1% (2023) and 0.4% (2024), well below trend (~1.5–
2%). Unemployment has drifted up from 3.7% to 4.4%. A 2% negative output gap suggests spare
capacity in the economy. A £30bn stimulus is roughly 1% of UK GDP.
AO3
AD = C + I + G + (X − M). Rising G directly increases AD. Additional rounds of spending via the
multiplier process (k = 1/(1−MPC) in a simple model, or 1/MPW in a more complete one) further
amplify the effect. Public infrastructure raises real GDP in the short run via increased demand, and
in the long run via higher productive capacity (LRAS shifts right). With spare capacity, rising AD will
mainly raise real output with limited inflationary pressure, moving the economy back towards trend
growth.
AO4
Counterarguments: (i) Time lags – infrastructure spending takes years to plan and deliver; the
stimulus may arrive after the output gap has closed naturally. (ii) Crowding out – if financed by
borrowing, higher gilt yields may raise interest rates, reducing private investment. (iii) Crowding out
is less likely when interest rates are low and there is significant spare capacity. (iv) Size of the
multiplier is uncertain and historically low in the UK (0.5–1.2). (v) Higher debt-to-GDP ratio may
require future tax rises, depressing future AD. (vi) Supply-side constraints – skills shortages in
construction may bid up wages/materials, causing cost-push pressures rather than real growth. (vii)
Leakages from imports reduce domestic multiplier effect. (viii) Whether growth is raised depends
also on exchange rate, monetary policy stance, and global conditions. Overall, the stimulus is likely
to boost growth in the short run, but its magnitude and sustainability are uncertain.

25. Discuss whether it is reasonable to conclude that Vietnam has a higher level of human
development than Botswana. [8]


 Ba                 AO2                                AO3                               AO4
 nd

  3                                      3 marks                            3 marks
                                         Excellent analysis                 Excellent evaluation
                                         A well-developed chain of          Clear judgements are made
                                         reasoning clearly explains         about which country has
                                         why one country has higher         higher human development,
                                         human development.                 or why this is not possible to
                                                                            tell.

  2    2 marks                           2 marks                            2 marks
       Good application                  Good analysis                      Good evaluation
       Data is used effectively to       Developed chain of reasoning       Developed counterarguments
       support argument.                 explaining higher human            present the opposing case.
                                         development in one country.

  1    1 mark                            1 mark                             1 mark
       Limited application               Limited analysis                   Limited evaluation
       Data referenced but use is        Some explanation given but         Counterarguments superficial
       superficial.                      arguments underdeveloped.          or unconvincing.

  0    0 marks                           0 marks                            0 marks
       No valid application.             No valid analysis.                 No valid evaluation.


Indicative content
AO2
Case FOR Vietnam having higher human development:
Higher HDI (0.726 vs 0.708) – on the composite index itself, Vietnam scores higher. Substantially
higher life expectancy (73.7 vs 61.1) – over 12 extra years of life expectancy suggests major
differences in health outcomes, healthcare systems or disease burden (Botswana has historically
suffered high HIV/AIDS prevalence). Slightly higher expected years of schooling (13.1 vs 12.2)
indicating a stronger education system for current pupils. Both classified 'High' HDI.
Case FOR Botswana having higher human development:
GNI/capita (PPP$) is substantially higher in Botswana ($16,780 vs $11,397) – almost 50% higher.
This affords higher private consumption of goods and services and greater government revenue for
public services. Higher mean years of schooling (9.9 vs 8.4) – reflects historical investment in
education.
AO3
Development is about improvements in well-being and quality of life, not just income. Chains of
reasoning should examine how each variable in the table contributes to welfare: GNI enables
private consumption (food, housing, leisure) and government provision; life expectancy captures
health outcomes including child mortality and nutrition; schooling (both mean and expected)
captures human capital accumulation. The HDI geometric-means these three dimensions, so a
single low dimension can dramatically reduce overall HDI (Botswana's life expectancy drags its HDI
down despite higher income).
AO4


                                 © Predicted Paper – in the style of WJEC/Eduqas
                                                        MARKING SCHEME – A520U10-1 (Predicted – Moderate)

Evaluation: (i) Evidence is mixed – Vietnam better on life expectancy and HDI; Botswana better on
income and mean schooling. (ii) The two countries' HDIs (0.726 vs 0.708) are very close and the
'high' threshold is arbitrary; both sit just above 0.7. (iii) HDI is a narrow measure – it excludes
inequality, poverty depth, gender gaps, environmental sustainability, political freedoms, governance
quality. Indicators such as the Inequality-adjusted HDI (IHDI), Multidimensional Poverty Index, or
Gini coefficient could give a fuller picture. (iv) Vietnam's lower income means more people may live
near subsistence despite higher life expectancy. (v) The Sen/capabilities approach suggests
development is about real freedoms to achieve valuable outcomes – this requires additional data.
(vi) Time dimension matters: Vietnam has grown rapidly from a low base, while Botswana has been
recovering from HIV/AIDS impacts. Overall, while Vietnam scores higher on the HDI, declaring its
development level 'higher' is a simplification; a complete judgement requires more data.
Allow other relevant points.


                               © Predicted Paper – in the style of WJEC/Eduqas
                                                         MARKING SCHEME – A520U10-1 (Predicted – Moderate)

26. Using supply and demand diagram(s), discuss whether the data suggests that coffee
prices are more likely to rise or fall in the future. [9]


 Ba                 AO1                               AO2                            AO3 / AO4
 nd

  3                                                                        3 marks (AO4)
                                                                           Well-reasoned supported
                                                                           judgement as to the most
                                                                           likely direction of coffee
                                                                           prices, or clear explanation of
                                                                           why the direction cannot be
                                                                           determined.

  2    2 marks                           2 marks                           2 marks (AO3)
       Accurate supply and demand        Effective use of the data on      Developed analysis of one
       diagrams are used to illustrate   both sides of the argument.       side (rising or falling prices). 2
       both sides of the argument.                                         marks (AO4)
                                                                           Developed counterargument
                                                                           for the other side.

  1    1 mark                            1 mark                            1 mark (AO3)
       Diagrams are present but          Effective use of data on one      Link between factors and
       have inaccuracies or cover        side of the argument only.        coffee prices but the chain is
       only one side of the debate.                                        underdeveloped. 1 mark
                                                                           (AO4)
                                                                           Counterargument present but
                                                                           not well developed.

  0    0 marks                           0 marks                           0 marks
       No understanding / diagram        No valid application.             No valid analysis or
       absent or entirely wrong.                                           evaluation.


Indicative content
AO1
Two supply-and-demand diagrams expected. Rising prices diagram: D shifts right (rising demand
from China, India) and/or S shifts left (drought in Brazil, higher production costs); new equilibrium at
higher P and uncertain Q. Falling prices diagram: D shifts left (global slowdown) and/or S shifts right
(robusta expansion); new equilibrium at lower P.
AO2
Factors pushing prices UP:
Severe droughts in Brazil reducing output – world's largest producer losing up to 30% of crop shifts
supply curve left. Rising demand from emerging markets (China, India) as incomes rise – coffee is a
normal good so YED > 0 drives the demand curve right. Climate change threatening long-run
arabica yields.
Factors pushing prices DOWN:
Expanding robusta production in Vietnam increases supply of substitute, which via the substitution
effect pulls down demand for arabica. Willingness to substitute indicates XED > 0. Global slowdown
reduces incomes and demand from cafés / food service.
AO3
Developed chain for rising prices: drought in Brazil reduces yields → supply curve shifts left → at
the old price there is excess demand → price bid up until new equilibrium reached with higher P and

                                © Predicted Paper – in the style of WJEC/Eduqas
                                                        MARKING SCHEME – A520U10-1 (Predicted – Moderate)

lower Q. Simultaneously rising incomes in emerging markets shift demand right, reinforcing upward
price pressure.
AO4
Counter and overall judgement: Robusta expansion and possible recession could offset the upward
pressures, at least partially. Arabica and robusta are imperfect substitutes – high-end consumers
may not accept robusta, limiting the downward pressure on arabica price. The chart shows prices
already recovering from ~165 cents (Dec 2022) to ~225 cents (Jun 2024) – market may already be
pricing in structural factors. Time horizon matters: short-run drought effects eventually reverse as
trees recover, but climate change is a long-run negative supply shock. PES is low in the short run
because coffee trees take 3–5 years to mature, making supply responses slow and price volatility
high. Overall, net pressure appears to be upward, especially in the long run, but significant
uncertainty remains.
Allow other relevant points.
